MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-17-2018 Continued To Mar-04-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-22-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Cross-defendants Comstock Homes Management 2, LLC and Robert Comstock's demurrer to the first cause of action for breach of contract in MBW-Brotherhood Way, LLC's third amended cross-complaint is overruled. A demurrer to a cause of action for breach of contract admits not only the contents of the contract, but also any pleaded meaning that the instrument is reasonably susceptible. (Aragon-Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc., (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239.) Paragraph 2 of the Management Services Agreement states that Comstock Homes hired MBW for Hawke's unique talents. Paragraphs 3 and 4 of the MSA provide that the agreement terminates if Hawke ceases to manage the project as contemplated under section 2 for any reason. Paragraph 4 of the MSA also sets forth other grounds for termination (e.g. breach and failure to cure within 10 days). MBW interprets these provisions to mean that the phrase "for any reason" does not include circumstances where Comstock Homes or RCS engages in misconduct and prevents MBW's management responsibilities. MBW alleges that its interpretation prevents the agreement from turning into an at-will agreement and ensures enforcement of the other termination provisions. MBW further alleges that Hawke was managing the project in the manner contemplated by the MSA and Comstock Homes wrongfully terminated the relationship. Lastly, MBW avers that Hawke only stopped managing the project months after September 30, 2014. These allegations cure the deficiencies identified in the order dated February 20, 2018. MBW now states a cause of action for breach of contract. Thrifty Payless, Inc. v. Mariners Mile Gateway, LLC (2010) 185 Cal.App.4th 1050 is distinguishable because it involved a motion for nonsuit-not a demurrer-and the court noted that the landlord continued to attempt to effectuate the agreement. (Id. at 1054.) Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/hk

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar May-17-2018 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-30-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Brent S. Colasurdo, Esq. of Umbert Zipser LLP, T:949.679.0052, for plaintiff/cross-defendant and moving party, on Court Call;
Andrew Weisberg, Esq. with Marc F. Feinstein, Esq. of O'Melvy & Myers LLP, T:213.430.7274, for defendant/cross complainant, on Court Call.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court modifies its tentative ruling on CROSS DEFENDANT COMSTOCK HOMES MANAGEMENT 2, LLC and ROBERT COMSTOCK's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. For reasons stated on the record, the demurrer is overruled as to the interference claim and sustained with leave to amend as to the breach of contract claim. Mr. Weisberg is to prepare and submit a proposed order that repeats the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, as stated on the record, and complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; mtorreano@sftc.org.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-11-2017 Continued To Nov-01-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-12-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Brent Colasurdo, Esq. (949-679-0052) of Umberg Zipser for Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Comstock Homes through Court Call.
Marc Feinstein, Esq. (213-430-7274) of O'Melveny & Myers LLP for Defendant/Cross-Complainant MBW Brotherhood Way through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant responds to demurrer on contract claim, Mr. Comstock was not acting for or on behalf of Comstock Homes. The Court inquires if Mr. Comstock is the sole member of LLC. Plaintiff responds. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling with modification as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANTS COMSTOCK HOMES MANAGEMENT 2, LLC and ROBERT COMSTOCK'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT OF MBW-BROTHERHOOD WAY LLC is SUSTAINED as to the first cause of action with twenty days leave to amend and sustained as to the second cause of action with twenty days leave to amend to allege additional facts referenced. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes)
As to the first cause of action for breach of contract, cross-complainant MBW-Brotherhood Way LLC does not allege any event constituting a breach of the Management Services Agreement (MSA). Contrary to MBW's position in opposition to the demurrer, the complaint, even liberally read, does not allege that the Comstock parties terminated the MSA before Mr. Hawke was removed from the project. MBW is given twenty days leave to amend to allege, if it can do so in good faith, that the MSA was terminated before Mr. Hawke was ever formally removed from the project. Defendant's Counsel to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/SRB)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-19-2017 Continued To Jul-12-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion Of Defendants Mbw Brotherhood Way, Llc And Christopher M. Hawke For Stay Of Proceedings Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure 1281.4
Off Calendar Apr-26-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendants MBW-Brotherhood Way, LLC and Christopher Hawke's motion for stay of proceeding pursuant to CCP 1281.4 is denied. This court's order filed October 27, 2016 in case 515256 was not an order compelling arbitration, but merely an order refusing, due to lack of any authority to do so, to stay an arbitration. Therefore, MBW and Mr. Hawke are not entitled to a mandatory stay per CCP 1281.4. Because this motion was not made on the grounds of the court's discretionary stay authority and plaintiffs have not had an opportunity to address the argument, the court does not consider the argument made for the first time in the reply memorandum that, per Seidman & Seidman v. Wolfson (1975) 50 Cal. App. 3d 826, this court should exercise its discretion to stay this case pending the arbitration between MBW and RCS. A discretionary stay motion is a different motion than a mandatory section 1281.4 motion with different requirements and different legal and equitable considerations. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk
